div1.entryFree:
Evidence, 1) witness, testimony, proof: thou art too fine in thy e. All's V, 3, 270. upon this e. H8 II, 1, 26. to be his e. now, Cymb. V, 5, 368. Plur. --s, Wint. V, 2, 41. bear e. against my soul, R3 I, 4, 67 (Ff. give e.). to give in e. Hml. III, 3, 64. give true e. to his love, Ant. I, 3, 74.
2) one that bears witness: his scarlet lust came e. to swear, Lucr. 1650 (== came as e.). Ado IV, 1, 38. Lr. III, 6, 37. Not inflected in the plur.: true e. of good esteem, H6B III, 2, 21. where are the e. R3 I, 4, 188 (Ff is the e.).
